{{Quote|IA hopes to recoup losses by reusing existing R5 shells in their moderately successful line of R2-AG and R4 agromech droids.|"IA Pulls R5's Plug"|<ref name="IA Pulls R5's Plug" />}}
The '''R2-AG agromech droid''',<ref name="IA Pulls R5's Plug" /> also known as the '''R2-AG4 agromech droid''',<ref name="Technical Journal" /> was a model of [[R-series/Legends|R-series]]<ref name="R series astromech droids" /> [[agromech droid]] manufactured by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]].<ref name="IA Pulls R5's Plug" /> A subseries of the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]] designed to work closely with [[Farm/Legends|farming]] equipment and [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] production machinery,<ref name="Technical Journal" /> the model was moderately successful. In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Industrial Automaton began reusing existing shells from their cancelled [[R5-series astromech droid]] in the production of R2-AG and [[R4 astromech droid/Legends|R4-series agromech droid]] lines in the hopes of recouping losses after a year of disappointing sales.<ref name="IA Pulls R5's Plug" /> However, these reshelled agromech [[Droid/Legends|droids]] did not sell very well.<ref name="The History of R-Series Astromech Droids">{{JournalCite|7|The History of R-Series Astromech Droids}}</ref>

R2-AGs were equipped with an [[Arc welder/Legends|arc welder]], a [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot]] [[Acoustic signaller/Legends|acoustic signaller]], retractable [[Manipulator arm/Legends|manipulator arms]], tripodal wheeled [[Limb/Legends|legs]], one of which was retractable, a [[Viewscreen/Legends|video display screen]], and a [[Photoreceptor/Legends|visual sensor]].<ref name="Technical Journal" />
